items.title: US CPI fell 0.4% in June, annual rate drops to 3.5% vs expected 3.8%
items.content: Consumer price index fell 0.4% MoM, biggest monthly decline since April 2020. Core CPI flat at 2.6% annual. Energy index slumped 5.7%. Markets rallied on the soft print.

items.title: Nasdaq, S&P 500 rally after softer-than-expected inflation data
items.content: Tech-led rally as CPI surprise eased rate concerns. VIX fell 3.85% to 16.50. 51.7% of stocks advancing. Fed September hike odds dropped to 63% from 75%.

items.title: Nasdaq leads US indexes higher; IBM holds gains in check
items.content: Major indexes higher after CPI relief. Dow had previously crossed 53000 for first time on July 6. Energy stocks continue outperformance.

items.title: Oil prices soar as Trump says US reinstating Strait of Hormuz blockade
items.content: Major indexes ended lower Monday as chip stocks dropped and oil surged on Hormuz blockade news. Geopolitical risk weighs on sentiment.

items.title: Hang Seng climbs 0.52% to 24,340.73 as China trade beat lifts sentiment
items.content: HSI rebounded 127pts to 24,340.73, third gain in four sessions. China's June trade data beat expectations. Basic materials and energy sectors led.

items.title: Hong Kong Hang Seng up 0.6% in late trade, lifted by materials and energy
items.content: HSI +0.6% in final hour, following China trade data beat. Energy sector continues strength amid oil price surge from Hormuz tensions.

items.title: US approves 3 more Chinese firms to buy advanced AI chips (Nvidia H200, AMD)
items.content: ZTE Kangxun, Maginfra cleared for Nvidia H200; Zhuhai Hengqin for AMD AI chips. Follows May approvals including Alibaba, Tencent, ByteDance. NVDA +3.5%, AMD +4%.

items.title: China memory chipmaker CXMT aims to raise $8.6B in Asia's biggest 2026 IPO
items.content: ChangXin Memory Technologies (CXMT) sets July 27 listing on Shanghai Stock Exchange. IPO price at 8.66 yuan per share.

items.title: Q2 earnings season kicks off: Big banks, ASML, TSMC, Netflix this week
items.content: JPMorgan, Bank of America, Goldman Sachs report Jul 14 before open. ASML Jul 15, TSMC Jul 16, Netflix Jul 16 after close. Heavy earnings week.

items.title: Energy stocks surge: Valero +83%, Marathon +86%, PBF +123% in 2026
items.content: Refiners and energy stocks massive YTD gains driven by oil price surge amid Hormuz crisis. Sector outperforming all others.

items.title: Basic Semi to raise prices up to 25% on strong AI and NEV demand
items.content: SiC power device maker Basic Semiconductor (9971.HK) adjusts prices from Q3. AI and new energy vehicle demand driving pricing power.

items.title: US and Iran threaten return to war after fiercest exchange since truce
items.content: Hormuz Strait shipping route dispute escalates. Iran wants northern passage control; US-sponsored southern route allegedly breaches ceasefire MoU. Trump threatens ceasefire is over.
